figures.caption: Fig. 1. Φ(x∗(p(t))) and ∥p(t)− p†∥2, under (5) with initial condition in intPc∗ . Trajectories depicted correspond to flows from the maximal and minimal sublevel sets across 100 initial conditions.

Fig. 4. ∥xk − x∗(pk)∥2 and ∥pk − p†∥2 under iteration (8), with initial condition x0 = (0,−0.5)⊤ and p0 = (−3,−3)⊤. Lines depict variations of the timescale separation degree βk/ak = O(k−γ) for different γ > 0.

Fig. 3. Iterates (xk, pk)k satisfying (8), from the initial condition x0 = (0,−0.5)⊤ and p0 = (−3,−3)⊤, and stepsize sequences ak = O(k−0.6) and βk = O(k−0.9). Solid lines indicate the boundaries ∂X and ∂Pc, with c = 0.95c∗. Dashed line indicates ∂Pc0 with c0 = 0.62c∗, which corresponds to the sublevel set on which p0 lies.

Fig. 2. Median (solid) and min–max envelope (shaded) of ∥xk−x∗(pk)∥2 (top row) and ∥pk − p†∥2 (bottom row) under iteration (8), with f = fNE (left column) and f = fBR (right column). Results represent 100 initial conditions sampled from intX × Pc, with c = 0.8c∗.
